questions.question: How much of the year does the prohibition of gas leaf blowers last? What date in the year does it start, and what date does it end?
questions.answer: The prohibition of gas leaf blowers lasts for approximately 4 months of the year. It starts on December 16 and ends on March 14.

questions.question: What are the permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation?
questions.answer: The permitted hours for gas leaf blower operation are Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. and Saturday from 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. All leaf-blower use is prohibited on Sundays, except by residents on their own property between 12:00 p.m. and 4:00 p.m. (See § 68-17).

questions.question: What are the penalties for violating gas leaf blower regulations?
Are there any exceptions for school/government/commercial properties?
How are violations reported and enforced?
questions.answer: Violations are enforced by holding the owner of the premises where the violation occurs, as well as the landscaper, gardener, contractor, or any other person who violates the article or is an accessory to the violation, liable for the violations. They are subject to the penalties set forth in Article III of Chapter 1 of the Code of the Village of Pelham, as stated in § 68-19.
